title: Seven Stars in Seven Pockets
synopsis: When Thistle the thorn fairy discovers a mysterious new neighbor leaving peculiar gifts around the planetarium during school visits, everything goes hilariously wrong as he tries to understand what each star-shaped present means.

pages.text: The third graders weren't supposed to trip over invisible fairy doors, but here we are. CRASH went Tommy's backpack into the planetarium's wall, and out tumbled Thistle, a tiny thorn fairy with mossy-green skin, pointed ears, wings like dried leaves, and a cap made from a chestnut husk. 'Great galoshes!' Thistle squeaked, his prickly nature showing immediately. 'Watch where you're stomping, you giant shoe-creatures!' But the children couldn't hear fairy voices, of course. They only heard Mrs. Peterson explaining how stars are born. Meanwhile, Thistle noticed something peculiar tucked behind the Big Dipper projector—a tiny silver star, no bigger than his thumbnail, wrapped in spider silk.

illustration.description: Treasure map style illustration on aged parchment showing a tiny thorn fairy with mossy-green skin, pointed ears, wings like dried leaves, and a cap made from a chestnut husk, tumbling from a backpack in a grand planetarium. Children sit in rows while stars swirl overhead. A small silver star gleams behind a projector. Sepia tones with jewel accents highlight the star.
illustration.alt: Thistle falling from a backpack in the planetarium during a school visit

pages.text: Thistle flew toward the mysterious gift, dodging laser beams that painted constellations on the dome above. ZAP! A holographic Saturn nearly knocked off his chestnut cap. 'Jumping juniper berries!' he yelped, spinning upside down. When he finally reached the star, he found a note written in acorn ink: 'For building dreams. Day 1 of 7.' Now, dear reader, you might think Thistle would be curious about who left such a thing. Instead, he was absolutely furious. 'Someone's in MY territory!' he huffed, his loyal heart feeling invaded. 'Probably some know-it-all firefly with fancy ideas!' The star began to hum a tiny tune, but Thistle was too busy grumbling to notice its magic.

illustration.description: Treasure map style illustration showing a tiny thorn fairy with mossy-green skin, pointed ears, wings like dried leaves, and a cap made from a chestnut husk, dodging holographic planets in the planetarium dome. A glowing silver star sits nearby with a small note. The scene uses aged parchment texture with dotted flight trails and compass roses, rendered in sepia tones with jewel-toned star accents.
illustration.alt: Thistle dodging holographic planets while approaching the mysterious star gift

pages.text: The second graders arrived like a stampede of elephants, and Thistle barely had time to hide his first star before—BONK!—a lunch box landed right on his hiding spot. 'Ow, ow, OW!' he cried, rubbing his pointy ears. But what's this? Another gift had appeared overnight, tucked inside the control booth where the planetarium operator pressed all those fascinating buttons. This star was copper-colored and shaped like a tiny gear. Its note read: 'For connecting ideas. Day 2 of 7.' Thistle's prickly personality flared up again. 'Two gifts? TWO?!' he sputtered. 'This mysterious neighbor is really pushing their luck!' He tried to grab the copper star, but it rolled away like a runaway marble, leading him on a wild chase between the seats.

illustration.description: Treasure map style illustration on aged parchment showing a tiny thorn fairy with mossy-green skin, pointed ears, wings like dried leaves, and a cap made from a chestnut husk, chasing a rolling copper gear-shaped star through planetarium seats filled with second graders. Hand-drawn map elements include dotted chase trails, compass roses, and sepia tones with copper jewel accents on the star.
illustration.alt: Thistle chasing a copper gear-star through the planetarium seats

pages.text: Round and round went the copper star, with Thistle flapping frantically behind it. WHOOSH past Mrs. Garcia's field trip group! ZOOM under little Sarah's sneakers! The star finally stopped when it bumped into—oh my!—a tiny door built into the planetarium's base that Thistle had never noticed before. It was perfectly fairy-sized, painted midnight blue with constellation patterns. 'Well, butter my wings,' Thistle whispered, forgetting to be grumpy for a moment. The copper star clicked into place like a key, and the door opened just a crack. Inside, Thistle glimpsed what looked like a workshop filled with miniature telescopes, star charts, and tools he'd never seen before. 'Someone's been VERY busy,' he muttered, his curiosity finally starting to outweigh his prickliness.

illustration.description: Treasure map style illustration showing a tiny thorn fairy with mossy-green skin, pointed ears, wings like dried leaves, and a cap made from a chestnut husk, discovering a small midnight-blue door decorated with constellations at the planetarium's base. The copper star acts as a key. Through the crack, miniature telescopes and tools are visible. Aged parchment texture with bold explorer cartography style, sepia tones, and jewel accents on the door and tools.
illustration.alt: Thistle finding a hidden fairy-sized door opened by the copper star

pages.text: Kindergarteners, as you probably know, are particularly good at creating chaos. They pointed at everything, dropped everything, and somehow managed to turn the volume dial ALL the way up just as Thistle was investigating his third gift. 'THE CONSTELLATION ORION—' BOOMED the speakers, launching poor Thistle like a popcorn kernel across the dome. When he finally landed (in a teacher's coffee cup, naturally), he spotted today's present: a golden star that looked remarkably like a tiny hammer. The note said: 'For shaping possibilities. Day 3 of 7.' Now Thistle was getting genuinely puzzled instead of just annoyed. 'What in the name of prickly pears is someone trying to build?' he wondered aloud, accidentally getting coffee foam on his leaf-wings.

illustration.description: Treasure map style illustration on aged parchment showing a tiny thorn fairy with mossy-green skin, pointed ears, wings like dried leaves, and a cap made from a chestnut husk, splashing in a coffee cup while kindergarteners point excitedly in the background. A golden hammer-shaped star sits nearby. The scene uses adventure cartography style with dotted flight paths, sepia tones, and golden jewel accents.
illustration.alt: Thistle landing in a coffee cup with kindergarteners in the background

pages.text: After drying his wings with a napkin (which tickled terribly), Thistle flew back to the mysterious door with his three stars. Click, click, CLICK! Each one fit perfectly into small holes around the door frame, and suddenly the workshop inside lit up with the softest starlight. What Thistle saw made his jaw drop to his tiny toes. Someone had been building a magnificent star projector, no bigger than a soup can, with intricate gears and mirrors and crystal lenses. Half-finished constellation maps covered the walls, and mechanical drawings showed plans for creating the most beautiful star shows a fairy had ever imagined. 'Leaping lichens,' Thistle breathed. 'Someone's not invading my territory—they're trying to make it more magical!' His loyal heart began to flutter with the first stirrings of friendship rather than suspicion.

illustration.description: Treasure map style illustration showing a tiny thorn fairy with mossy-green skin, pointed ears, wings like dried leaves, and a cap made from a chestnut husk, peering into the illuminated fairy workshop. A miniature star projector sits on a workbench surrounded by constellation maps and mechanical drawings. Three stars glow in the door frame. Aged parchment texture with hand-drawn map elements, sepia tones, and jewel accents on the projector and stars.
illustration.alt: Thistle discovering the magical workshop with a miniature star projector

pages.text: By Sunday (after collecting a crystal star for 'focusing light,' an iron star for 'supporting dreams,' a wooden star for 'growing patience,' and a pearl star for 'polishing hope'), Thistle was practically bouncing with excitement. No more school groups today—just him and the mysterious gift-giver he was finally ready to meet. He arranged all seven stars in their proper places around the door, and KER-CHUNK! The workshop opened completely. Out stepped the tiniest fairy Thistle had ever seen—smaller even than himself—with wings like soap bubbles and fingers stained with stardust. 'Hello,' she said shyly. 'I'm Nova. I've been trying to build something wonderful for this place, but I wasn't sure if the resident fairy would approve. So I thought... maybe gifts first?'

illustration.description: Treasure map style illustration on aged parchment showing a tiny thorn fairy with mossy-green skin, pointed ears, wings like dried leaves, and a cap made from a chestnut husk, meeting an even tinier fairy with soap-bubble wings and stardust-stained fingers. Seven different stars glow around an open workshop door. The scene uses treasure map cartography with compass roses, bold explorer style, sepia tones, and multiple jewel accents for each star.
illustration.alt: Thistle meeting Nova the tiny fairy outside the fully opened workshop

pages.text: Thistle's prickly exterior melted completely. 'You built all this... for the planetarium?' Nova nodded eagerly. 'I love how the children's eyes light up when they see the stars. But I thought—what if we could make private shows for the mice and moths and other small creatures who visit at night? What if we could build magic for everyone?' Together, they activated Nova's incredible star projector. Tiny constellations danced across the workshop walls, telling stories in languages that only existed in starlight. 'Well,' said Thistle, his loyal heart now completely won over, 'I suppose one fairy territory can definitely hold two fairies—especially when one of them is the finest builder in seven galaxies!' And from that day forward, dear reader, the planetarium held twice as much magic as before, though the school children never knew why the stars seemed to twinkle just a little more brightly during their visits.

illustration.description: Treasure map style illustration showing a tiny thorn fairy with mossy-green skin, pointed ears, wings like dried leaves, and a cap made from a chestnut husk, working alongside a soap-bubble-winged fairy in the magical workshop. Their star projector creates dancing constellations on the walls while the planetarium dome sparkles overhead. Aged parchment with adventure cartography elements, dotted trails connecting the stars, sepia tones, and brilliant jewel accents throughout the magical scene.
illustration.alt: Thistle and Nova working together with their star projector creating magical constellations

coverPrompt.description: A treasure map style illustration showing a tiny thorn fairy with mossy-green skin, pointed ears, wings like dried leaves, and a cap made from a chestnut husk, standing inside a grand planetarium dome filled with swirling stars. The scene is rendered on aged parchment with sepia tones, compass roses in the corners, and dotted trails leading to seven glowing star-shaped objects scattered around the domed ceiling.
coverPrompt.alt: Thistle the thorn fairy in a star-filled planetarium with mysterious gifts

questions.question: What kind of fairy is Thistle?
questions.explanation: Thistle is a thorn fairy with mossy-green skin and prickly personality!

questions.question: How many gifts does Nova leave for Thistle?
questions.explanation: Nova leaves seven star-shaped gifts, one for each day of the week!

questions.question: What is Nova building in her workshop?
questions.explanation: Nova builds a miniature star projector to create magical shows for small creatures!

questions.question: At first, how does Thistle feel about the mysterious gifts?
questions.explanation: Thistle's prickly nature makes him angry that someone is in 'his' territory at first!

questions.question: What opens Nova's workshop door?
questions.explanation: All seven star-shaped gifts work together like puzzle pieces to open the workshop door!
